channel.title: Project on Built by Nikhil
channel.description: Recent content in Project on Built by Nikhil

item.title: Day 6: Build a Traffic Light System
item.description: Day 6 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a full red-yellow-green traffic light with realistic timing, then add a pedestrian button that overrides the cycle.

item.title: Day 10: Project — Smart Night Light
item.description: Day 10 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a smart night light that turns on automatically when it gets dark, with a manual override button.

item.title: Day 14: Project — Auto Fan System
item.description: Day 14 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build an automatic fan that turns on when the room gets too bright or too hot, with a buzzer alert. Everything from Days 1-13 in one project.

item.title: Day 18: Project — Mini Weather Station
item.description: Day 18 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a mini weather station that shows light level, simulated temperature, and custom sun and moon icons on the LCD.

item.title: Day 22: Project — Smart Room System
item.description: Day 22 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the big one. Build a Smart Room System that combines IR, sound, LCD, light sensor, LED, and buzzer into one coherent program.

item.title: Day 23: Pick Your Project — Brainstorm and Plan
item.description: Day 23 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the first day of your final project. Brainstorm ideas, pick one that excites you, and sketch the system diagram before writing any code.

item.title: Day 24: Build Your Project — One Piece at a Time
item.description: Day 24 of Learn Coding with Arduino — take the project plan from Day 23 and start building. One module at a time, tested and working, before moving to the next.

item.title: Day 25: Integrate and Debug
item.description: Day 25 of Learn Coding with Arduino — stitch all the modules from Day 24 into one coherent sketch, break loop() into helper functions, and debug integration issues.

item.title: Day 26: Demo Day — Show It Off
item.description: Day 26 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the final day. You present your project, celebrate the journey, and think about what to build next.
